questions.question: "Drain of Wealth" theory was proposed by-
questions.explanation: "Drain of Wealth" was proposed by Dadabhai Naoroji in his book "Poverty and Un-British Rule in India" (1867). He blamed it on British policies for Indian economic ills. It refers to a portion of national product of India which was not available for consumption to its people. This was due to the extortion by company servants from the fortunes of rulers, zamindars, merchants and common people, purchasing goods out of revenue from Bengal and sending them home, duty free trade provided to British companies etc.

questions.question: The first English factory in India came up in which among the following places?
questions.explanation: Captain William Hawkins came to the court of Jahangir in 1609 and asked for permission to open a factory in India. Jahangir gave permission to open one in Surat and the first English factory came up there in 1613

questions.question: The Treaty of Purandhar was signed in the year-
questions.explanation: During the reign of Aurangzeb, Raja Jai Singh of Amber was pointed in order to put down Shivaji, the Maratha Ruler, who challenged the Mughal Empire. Jai Singh signed the Treat of Purandhar in 1665 with Shivaji and according to this, Shivaji ceded some forts to the Mughals and paid a visit to the Mughal court in Agra

questions.question: Who among the following is the last Mughal Emperor to have ruled India?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah II was the last Mughal Emperor to have ruled India and he ruled from 1837 AD to 1857 AD. He was made the premier during the revolt of 1857 AD. At the end, he was deported to Rangoon in 1858 and died there in the year 1862

questions.question: Din-i-Ilahi was proclaimed by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: In order to protest against the orthodoxy and bigotry of religious practices prevalent at the time, Akbar proclaimed a new religion named as Din-i-Ilahi in the year 1581 AD. It was based on the synthesis of values taken from several religions like Hinduism, Jainism and Christianity. It did not recognize the Prophet, though. Birbal was the only Hindu who followed the religion and it failed to garner much interest among the masses

questions.question: The autobiography of Babur was translated into English by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: The autobiography of Babur, Tuzuk-i-Babri was composed by him in Turki and it gives an excellent account of India and his empire in India. It was translated into Persian by Abdur Rahim Khanekhana (named as Baburnama) and was translated into English by Madam Bedridge

questions.question: 'Kavitawali' was authored by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: Tulsidas was one the greatest Bhakti saint who made it possible for the Bhakti movement to spread its wings. He was the greatest saint-poet of the Ram Bhakti cult of Vaishanavism and also the celebrated author of classics such as 'Ramcharitamanas', 'Kavitawali' and 'Gitawali' among others

questions.question: Charminar in Hyderabad was built by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Quli Qutub Shah is known as the greatest ruler of the Qutub Shahi Dynasty and it was he who founded the city of Hyderabad, originally known as Bhagyanagar after the name of the Sultan's favourite, Bhagyamati. He was instrumental in building Charminar in Hyderabad

questions.question: Who authored the book 'Fatawa-i-Jahandari'?
questions.explanation: Some literatrure of Delhi Sultanate and the writers: • Tahqiq-i-Hind: Alberuni • Tabaqat-i-Nasiri: Minhaj-us-Siraj • Laila Majnu: Amir Khusrau • Khazain-ul-Futuh: Amir Khusrau • Fatwah-i-Firoz-Shahi: Firoz Shah • Jawahar-fil-Jawahir: Alberuni • Tughlaq-Nama: Amir Khusrau • Nuh-Siphir: Amir Khusaru

questions.question: Who was the ruler of India when Timur the Lame invaded India?
questions.explanation: Timur the Lame was a Turkish Chief and a cruel conqueror from Mongolia and also was a descendant of Chengiz Khan. He invaded India in the year 1398 AD during the reign of Muhammad Shah Tughlaq, the last ruler of Tughlaq Dynasty. He plundered Delhi and left Khizir Khan to rule Punjab on his behalf before returning to Central Asia

questions.question: Which among the following was the real name of Muhammad Bin Tughlaq?
questions.explanation: Prince Jauna, the son of Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq ascended the throne of Delhi Sultanate in the year 1325 AD and he was best known as Muhammad Bin Tughlaq. During his reign, he initiated five administrative reforms and they were- taxation in the doab region to increase the revenue from agriculture, transfer of capital from Delhi to Devagiri in the South, introduction of Token currency, Khurassan expedition and Qarachil expedition

questions.question: Who introduced the system of Dagh in his Army during the reign of Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khilji was the greatest ruler of the Khilji dynasty and ruled from 1296 AD to 1316 AD in the Delhi Sultanate. He initiated a number of administrative reforms and among them were Dagh and Cherhra. The first referred to the branding of horses used in the Army and the second was related to the descriptive roll of the soldiers in the Army

questions.question: Who among the following rulers of the Delhi Sultanate was given the title of Lakh Baksh?
questions.explanation: Qutubuddin Aibak was the first ruler of the Delhi Sultanate and he established the slave dynasty in Delhi in 1206 AD. He died in 1210 AD while playing Chaugan or Polo. He built two mosques- Quwat-ul-Islam in Delhi and Adhai Din ka Jhonpra in Ajmer. He also began the construction of Qutub Minar in the honour of Sufi Saint Khwaja Qutubuddin Bakhtiyar Kafi

questions.question: The first ever Muslim Invasion in India took place in the year-
questions.explanation: Mohammad Bin Qasim invaded India in the year 712 AD and that was the first ever invasion by any Muslim ruler in India. He conquered the province of Sindh and made it a part of the empire of Omayyad Khilafat. After that, Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni invaded India 17 times and his was the first Turk invasion to India. In 1025 AD, he attacked and plundered the most celebrated Hindu temple of Somnath in Gujarat and destroyed it in 1026 AD

questions.question: Who authored the book 'Kavirajmarga'?
questions.explanation: Amoghvarsha was one of the greatest rulers in the Rsahtrakuta Dynasty that ruled in South India. He ruled for 64 years but by temperament, he was more inclined to literature than war and expansion. He was an author and wrote Kavirajmarga, the earliest Kannada Book on Poetics

questions.question: The Battle of Chandawar took place in the year-
questions.explanation: Battle of Chadawar was fought between Mohammad Ghori and Jai Chand Gadhwal, who was the last Rajputa King to be defeated and killed by the Muslim ruler. It was the end of the Rajputa Kingdom in India whereas Muslim rule was on the verge of beginning

questions.question: Prayaga Prasasti was composed by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: Allahabad Pillar Inscriptions / Prayaga Prasasti is the most detailed and authentic record of the reign of the greatest Gupta ruler Samudragupta. It was composed by his court poet Harisena. According to this, he was a great ruler and annexed most parts of the Gangetic Valley and Central India but did not do the same for South India where he remained content with victories only

questions.question: Which among the following is known as the Bible of the Tamil Land?
questions.explanation: Kural or Muppal is a part of Patinenkilakanakku and was written by Tiruvalluvar. It is also known as the Bible of the Tamil Land. The book is a treatise on polity, ethics and social issues. This is a fine example of the Sangam literature which was prevalent in Southern India only

questions.question: We know about the Sunga Dynasty which started after the fall of the Mauryas. The capital of the Sunga Dynasty was in where among the following places?
questions.explanation: Sunga Dynasty was established by Pushyamitra Sunga, a Brahmin commander in chief in the court of the last Mauryan ruler Brihadratha in 185 BC. He was a staunch supporter of Hinduism. He was succeeded by his son Agnimitra after whom a number weak rulers came and went. Vihar, Chaitya, Stupa of Bhaja, Amravati Stupa, Nasik Chaitya are some of the examples of the Sunga Art

questions.question: The Ashokan Edicts were deciphered by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: Ashoka was one of the greatest rulers of India and we get to know so much about him from his rock edicts and inscriptions. There are rock edicts, pillar edicts, cave inscriptions located at several places in the Indian sub continent. Their importance came to be known only after they were deciphered by James Princep in the year 1837 and it was established that Ashoka was the author of these inscriptions. Prakrit was the language used in these edicts but the script was either in Khariosthi or Greek or Armanic or Brahmi depending on the place of the inscription

questions.question: Which of the following texts describes Chandragupta's victory over the Nandas with the assistance of Chanakya?
questions.explanation: Vishakha Dutta's Mudra Rakshasa describes the way Chandragupta Maurya defeated Dhananada to win the empire of Magadha though it was written during the reign of the Mauryas. Besides this, it gives an excellent account of the prevailing socio economic condition of the age

questions.question: Nalanada University was established by whom among the following rulers?
questions.explanation: Some Buddhist Universities and their founders: • Odantapuri: Gopala • Vikramshila: Dharmapala • Somapuri: Dharmapala • Jagadal: Ramapala • Vallabhi: Bhattaraka

questions.question: 1st Buddhist Council was held in the year-
questions.explanation: 1st Buddhist Council was held in the year 483 BC and the venue was Rajagriha. It was during the reign of Ajatasatru of Haryanka dynasty and the chairman was Mahakassapa. During this convention, compilation of Sutta Pitaka and Vinaya Pitaka by Ananda and Upali took place

questions.question: Every great event in Gautama Buddha's life is characterized by a particular symbol. Which among the following represents his Mahabhinishkramana (Renunciation)?
questions.explanation: There are basically five great events in the life of Gautama Buddha and each of them is characterized by a symbol. His birth is characterized by lotus and bull, his renunciation of worldly affairs is represented by horse, his enlightenment, by Bodhi Tree, his first sermon is by Wheel and his death (Mahaparinirvana) by Stupa

questions.question: Who among the following ancient Indian Kings was known as Seniya because he was the first Indian King to possess a standing army?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara, the founder of the Haryanka dynasty in Magadha, was known as Seniya and also Shronika. He ruled from 544 BC to 492 BC and was a contemporary of Gautama Buddha. He built the new city of Rajagriha

questions.question: Who among the following is considered as the founder of Yoga Darshana, an important school of Indian philosophy?
questions.explanation: There are basically six schools of Indian philosophy which are known as Shad-Darshanas. They are Sankhya Darshana, Yoga Darshana, Nyaya Darshana, Vaiseshika Darshana, Purva Mimansa and Uttar Mimansa. The founders are Kapila, Patanjali, Akshapada Gautama, Uluka Kanada, Jaimini and Badarayana respectively

questions.question: Which of the following is the oldest of the Puranic Texts?
questions.explanation: The Puranas are considered to be important examples of Vedic literature. It means the old. There are 18 famous Puranas out of which Matsya Purana is the oldest whereas the others are Bhagavata Purana, Vayu Purana, Brahmanda Purana etc. They describe genealogies of various royal dynasties

questions.question: Which of the following is considered as the oldest of the four Vedas?
questions.explanation: Vedic literature is mainly based on Vedas, Brahamans, Aryanakas and Upanishads. Out of the four Vedas, Rig Veda is the oldest and it is basically a collection of lyrics. Sama Veda is important for Indian music, Yajur Veda is based on sacrificial prayers whereas Atharva Veda is based on magical formulae

questions.question: In which of the following Harappan sites, the remains of a dockyard have been discovered?
questions.explanation: Lothal was situated at the bank of the river Bhogava in the district of Ahmadabad in Gujarat. It was discovered in the year 1954 by SR Rao. The things that have been found at this site are dockyard, rice husk, metal workers', shell-ornament makers' and bead-makers' shops, five altars, terracotta figurine of a horse, double burial etc

questions.question: Which among the following is considered as the duration of the Indus Valley Civilization?
questions.explanation: Harappan Civilization or Indus Valley Civilization is believed to have spanned over the years 2500-1750 BC. It has been decided according to the result of the Carbon 14 dating test. The civilization was spread over Sindh, Baluchistan, Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Gujarat, Western UP and Northern Maharashtra
